channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: DOJ memo stokes fear among disability advocates of a return to institutionalization
item.description: The Justice Department's opinion challenges civil rights protections that have long treated the institutionalization of disabled Americans as a last resort.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department's opinion challenges civil rights protections that have long treated the institutionalization of disabled Americans as a last resort.

(Image credit: Patrick Semansky)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Judy Blume says she's done writing: '50 years is enough!'
item.description: Judy Blume wrote her last book more than a decade ago. At the Santa Fe International Literary Festival, NPR's Scott Simon talked to Blume about her long career and why she doesn't miss writing.
item.content:encoded:
Judy Blume wrote her last book more than a decade ago. At the Santa Fe International Literary Festival, NPR's Scott Simon talked to Blume about her long career and why she doesn't miss writing.

(Image credit: Tira Howard Photography.)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: These nuns spent a lifetime helping others. In their last years, who will help them?
item.description: The sisters of Uganda are teachers, health-care advocates and more. Those who are in their twilight of their life need help. Who will come to their aid?
item.content:encoded:
The sisters of Uganda are teachers, health-care advocates and more. Those who are in their twilight of their life need help. Who will come to their aid?

(Image credit: Stuart Tibaweswa for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sophie Neiman

item.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz shut as U.S.-Iran talks set for Sunday in Switzerland
item.description: U.S. and Iranian teams are set to hold "technical-level" talks, according to Pakistan's foreign ministry. But fighting in Lebanon and claims over the Strait of Hormuz threaten a tentative agreement.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. and Iranian teams are set to hold "technical-level" talks, according to Pakistan's foreign ministry. But fighting in Lebanon and claims over the Strait of Hormuz threaten a tentative agreement.

(Image credit: Hassan Ammar)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Palestinian-American kids find inspiration, and escape, on the soccer pitch
item.description: A dozen miles away from the World Cup games in New York/ New Jersey Stadium, Palestinian-American kids turn to soccer as an escape from the realities of war.
item.content:encoded:
A dozen miles away from the World Cup games in New York/ New Jersey Stadium, Palestinian-American kids turn to soccer as an escape from the realities of war.

(Image credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: This couple got married at the end of Romeo and Juliet — and so will 31 others
item.description: At Free Shakespeare in the Park in New York, real weddings are happening every night after a production of Romeo and Juliet. But don't those characters die?
item.content:encoded:
At Free Shakespeare in the Park in New York, real weddings are happening every night after a production of Romeo and Juliet. But don't those characters die?

(Image credit: Rebecca J Michelson)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: A California man's case highlights gaps in care and oversight at DHS detention centers
item.description: Federal officers shot Ricardo Parias eight months ago during an ICE operation to detain him. His lawyer says he is still in pain, highlighting gaps in oversight and care in DHS facilities.
item.content:encoded:
Federal officers shot Ricardo Parias eight months ago during an ICE operation to detain him. His lawyer says he is still in pain, highlighting gaps in oversight and care in DHS facilities.

(Image credit: Office of Los Angeles City Council member Curren Price)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: James Burrows, director of classic shows 'Cheers' and 'Friends,' dies at 85
item.description: Burrows spent his career behind the camera specializing in situation comedies. Few viewers recognized him or knew his name, other than to see it flash quickly on the screen in the opening credits. But they knew his work.
item.content:encoded:
Burrows spent his career behind the camera specializing in situation comedies. Few viewers recognized him or knew his name, other than to see it flash quickly on the screen in the opening credits. But they knew his work.

(Image credit: Jason LaVeris)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A new study rewrites the history of the plague
item.description: A new study looks at one of the most prolific pathogens in human history — the plague.
item.content:encoded: A new study looks at one of the most prolific pathogens in human history — the plague.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Air Force One, gifted to Trump from Qatar, arrives at Joint Base Andrews
item.description: The luxury Boeing 747, initially valued at $400 million, arrived ahead of schedule on Friday. The jet caused controversy as one of the biggest foreign gifts ever received by the U.S. government.
item.content:encoded:
The luxury Boeing 747, initially valued at $400 million, arrived ahead of schedule on Friday. The jet caused controversy as one of the biggest foreign gifts ever received by the U.S. government.

(Image credit: Alex Wong
)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger
